title: willoughbya gracilis
intro: Willoughbya gracilis, also known as the New Zealand fan flower, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, Willoughbya gracilis is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be applied topically to the skin as a poultice or ointment.
